items.caption: An example of acid mine drainage. it shows a close-up of a stream with a yellow-orange color, indicating the presence of sulfuric acid. the surrounding rocks are rich in sulfide minerals, which, when exposed to water and air, react to form sulfuric acid. this acid can dissolve other harmful metals and metalloids, such as arsenic, from the surrounding rock. the acid mine drainage can have severe impacts on animals and plants, causing them to have a ph of 4 or lower, similar to battery acid.
